platforms.name: Serve Robotics RS2
platforms.notes: Sidewalk delivery robot operating in Los Angeles and other US cities. Partnerships with Uber Eats and 7-Eleven.

failure_modes.symptom: audible grinding from drivetrain, vibration spike at low speed, increased motor current draw by 15-25% above baseline
failure_modes.root_cause: bearing race spalling from water ingress via inadequate IP sealing after repeated curb impact events; grit contamination accelerates race wear

repair_protocol.title: Drive Hub Motor Bearing Replacement

steps.action: Power down robot via fleet management console; engage software lockout; verify zero-energy state on dash LED
steps.warning: Capacitor discharge — wait 2 minutes after power-off before touching drive electronics

steps.action: Remove 6x M5 wheel mounting bolts; extract full wheel/hub assembly from chassis dropouts
Remove hub motor end cap retaining snap ring; carefully extract rotor from stator housing; label motor phase wires
steps.tool: snap ring pliers, heat gun at 60°C to soften thread-lock
steps.warning: Do not stress motor phase wires — they are short-bonded to stator windings

steps.action: Press out degraded bearing from rotor carrier using bearing press; inspect stator for corrosion deposits or winding discoloration
steps.tool: hydraulic bearing press, inspection light, magnifying glass

steps.action: Clean bore with isopropyl alcohol; apply 2-3 drops Loctite 648 retaining compound; press new NTN 6203-2RS sealed bearing to full seat depth
steps.warning: Do not spin bearing until adhesive cures 30 minutes at 22°C

steps.action: Inspect and replace IP65 end cap gasket; apply thin bead of Dow Corning 732 RTV to mating face; reassemble rotor; torque end cap bolts to 12 Nm
steps.tool: torque wrench 0-25 Nm, Dow Corning 732 RTV sealant

steps.action: Reinstall wheel assembly; hand-tighten then torque M5 bolts to 8 Nm in star pattern
Remove software lockout; run 10-minute drive cycle on flat surface; verify current draw <±10% of factory baseline; confirm vibration RMS <0.4g on IMU

parts.part_name: Hub Motor End Cap IP65 Gasket Kit

failure_modes.symptom: point cloud density drops >30%, phantom obstacle detections in clear path, robot stops unexpectedly or navigates erratically
failure_modes.root_cause: lens window contamination from road grime, rain, and exhaust particulates; secondary failure mode is bearing wear in rotating scanner head after continuous 24/7 operation

repair_protocol.title: Lidar Lens Cleaning and Scanner Health Check

steps.action: Park robot in safe off-street location; engage lockout via fleet console
Remove lidar protective dome (4x M3 captive screws, quarter-turn); expose lens window
steps.warning: Do not touch lens glass — handle dome by edge only

steps.action: Apply 2 sprays of Optically Clear Lens Cleaning Solution to lint-free microfibre cloth; wipe lens in single radial strokes from center outward; repeat until surface is haze-free
steps.tool: microfibre cloth, Zeiss Lens Care spray
steps.warning: Never use paper towels or circular wiping — scratches anti-reflection coating

steps.action: Inspect rotating head: spin by hand with power off; confirm smooth rotation, no grinding or notchiness; check motor flex cable for fraying
Reinstall dome; power on; run diagnostic scan tool to measure point cloud return rate — must be >92% of factory baseline
If point cloud recovery <92% after cleaning, replace full lidar module per manufacturer RMA procedure
steps.warning: Full module replacement requires re-calibration of sensor-to-chassis transform — do not skip calibration step

failure_modes.symptom: reduced range >20% below spec, battery thermal event warning, cells failing to balance above 90% SOC
failure_modes.root_cause: cell imbalance from repeated partial-charge cycles and thermal cycling in ambient temperatures <5°C or >40°C; accelerated by vibration-induced micro-shorts in 18650/21700 cell holders

repair_protocol.title: Battery Pack Cell Balance Inspection and BMS Reset

steps.action: Park robot; connect to fleet console; pull BMS telemetry report — identify cells with voltage delta >50mV from pack median
Perform forced balance charge: set BMS to slow-charge mode (0.2C); allow 8-hour passive balancing cycle
If delta persists >80mV post-balance, remove battery pack (4x M6 bolts, 2 Anderson connector locks)
steps.warning: Wear insulated gloves; pack voltage is 48V nominal — arc flash risk

steps.action: Transfer pack to bench; connect to Texas Instruments BQ78350 BMS evaluation board; run full cell individual voltage sweep; flag cells <3.0V or >4.22V
steps.tool: TI BQ78350 evaluation kit, DMM with cell-probe adapter

steps.action: Replace degraded cell modules (hot-swap modules if pack design permits); update BMS firmware to latest release via SPI flash tool
steps.warning: Cell replacement in Li-ion packs is advanced — refer to certified battery technician if >3 cells flagged

steps.action: Reinstall pack; run 3-cycle capacity test (full charge/discharge); confirm capacity >85% of nameplate; log result in fleet system

parts.part_name: Texas Instruments BQ78350 BMS Replacement IC

failure_modes.symptom: lens condensation causing blurred perception output, camera frames reported as 'low confidence' by vision stack, increased pedestrian detection latency
failure_modes.root_cause: thermal differential between cold ambient and warm robot body causes moisture condensation on exterior lens surfaces; hydrophobic coating degradation after 6-12 months outdoor exposure

repair_protocol.title: Camera Lens Defogging and Hydrophobic Recoat

steps.action: Identify affected cameras from fleet diagnostic image quality scores; flag cameras with mean SSIM <0.75 versus clear reference
Power down affected camera module; remove housing cover (3x M2.5 screws)
steps.warning: Fragile ribbon cable at camera module — do not stress

steps.action: Apply Rain-X Original Glass Treatment to exterior lens surface with cotton applicator; buff to clear with microfibre cloth; allow 5 minutes cure
steps.tool: Rain-X Original, cotton applicator, microfibre cloth
steps.warning: Keep Rain-X away from rubber gaskets — causes swelling

steps.action: Inspect IP gasket around lens housing; replace if any cracking or compression set visible; apply thin bead of Dow Corning 732 to seal
Reassemble housing; power on; run image quality check — confirm SSIM >0.9 and vision stack confidence >0.85

failure_modes.symptom: robot stops mid-delivery and enters safe-stop mode, fleet console shows 'connection lost', delayed return-to-home behavior
failure_modes.root_cause: cellular modem firmware crash triggered by malformed network registration packets from carrier edge nodes; secondary cause is antenna connector corrosion from humidity ingress loosening SMA connector impedance match

repair_protocol.title: Cellular Modem Firmware Update and Antenna Inspection

steps.action: Connect to robot via local WiFi or physical Ethernet port (maintenance port under belly panel)
SSH into robot OS; check modem status: `mmcli -m 0 --status`; confirm if modem shows 'failed' or 'searching' state
Attempt modem soft reset: `mmcli -m 0 --reset`; wait 60 seconds; re-check status
If persistent, flash latest modem firmware via fleet OTA command or USB tether: `fwupd upgrade --modem`
steps.warning: Do not interrupt power during firmware flash — will brick modem

steps.action: Inspect SMA antenna connectors (2x on modem module): check torque at 0.5 Nm; clean contacts with DeoxIT D5; re-seat connectors
steps.tool: SMA torque wrench 0.5Nm, DeoxIT D5 spray

steps.action: Run 30-minute connectivity soak test via fleet console; confirm 0 disconnections and latency <200ms

parts.part_name: SMA Female Connector IP67 Panel Mount (replacement)

failure_modes.symptom: visible cracking at wheel arch gussets, frame flex under payload, audible creak over bumps
failure_modes.root_cause: repeated curb drop impacts (5-15mm height) exceeding design fatigue load at chassis tube weld joints; aluminum alloy 6061-T6 weld heat-affected zone embrittlement

repair_protocol.title: Chassis Crack Inspection and Gusset Plate Reinforcement

steps.action: Remove all body panels; perform full visual inspection of chassis tube welds with 10x magnification loupe; mark any cracks with paint pen
steps.tool: 10x magnification loupe, paint pen, inspection light

steps.action: Photograph all crack locations for engineering review before any repair; submit to fleet engineering Slack channel
steps.warning: Do not operate robot with confirmed chassis crack — retire from service immediately

steps.action: If crack is <10mm at non-load-bearing panel: clean area with acetone; apply JB Weld Aluminum Epoxy across crack; clamp and cure 24h
steps.tool: acetone, JB Weld Aluminum Epoxy, clamps
steps.warning: JB Weld structural repair is field-expedient only — engineering sign-off required before return to service

steps.action: For weld joint cracks: robot must be returned to depot; certified welder performs TIG re-weld and 2mm aluminum gusset plate bonding with Scotch-Weld 2216
steps.warning: Field welding of 6061-T6 is not recommended without post-weld heat treatment — depot repair only

steps.action: Post-repair load test: apply full 15kg payload; drive over standard curb-drop test (80mm) 10 times; re-inspect all welds

predictive_signals.signal_source: IMU left vs right accelerometer delta
IMU shock event counter in fleet log

failure_modes.symptom: thermal throttling warning in fleet console, motor speed limited to 50% of commanded value, controller reboots mid-run
failure_modes.root_cause: controller MOSFET junction overheating due to clogged thermal pad contact between MOSFET and heatsink, compounded by increased bearing drag increasing motor load; thermal runaway risk above 85°C junction temp

repair_protocol.title: Motor Controller Thermal Interface Replacement

steps.action: Power down robot; wait 5 minutes for controller thermal soak before opening enclosure
steps.warning: Controller heatsink may be at 70°C+ immediately after shutdown — burn hazard

steps.action: Remove controller enclosure cover (6x M3 screws); photograph thermal pad condition and PCB state
Remove 4x M3 heatsink mounting screws; separate heatsink from PCB; note old thermal pad condition (compression, dry-out, cracking)
Clean both mating surfaces with isopropyl alcohol; allow to dry fully
Apply new Fujipoly XR-m or Bergquist GP3000S30 thermal pad (3mm thick, cut to MOSFET cluster footprint); remove protective liners; seat heatsink; torque M3 screws to 0.8 Nm
Reassemble enclosure; power on; run motor at 100% load for 10 minutes; verify controller temperature plateau <70°C via fleet telemetry

parts.part_name: Thermal Interface Screw Kit M3x8 SS

platforms.name: Starship Gen3
platforms.notes: 6-wheel sidewalk delivery robot with 12 cameras for 360-degree perception. Deployed across US universities and cities.

failure_modes.symptom: one wheel not tracking speed commands, robot yaws unexpectedly, fleet console shows wheel speed encoder mismatch
failure_modes.root_cause: drive belt stretch and tooth skip on rear idler pulleys after 600-800 hours of operation; grit ingress from sidewalk grime accelerates belt tooth wear; encoder magnet displacement from vibration

repair_protocol.title: Drive Belt Inspection, Tension Adjustment, and Encoder Realignment

steps.action: Park robot; engage lockout; remove lower chassis belly panel (8x M4 screws)
Inspect each of 6 drive belts for: tooth missing, cracking, glazing, or lateral tracking offset >2mm from pulley center
Measure belt tension using Gates Sonic Tension Meter — target 80-95 Hz frequency for 8mm wide HTD3M belt
For belts out of spec: loosen idler pulley lock nut; adjust idler to achieve target tension; re-lock at 6 Nm
Replace belts with visible tooth damage: remove old belt; clean pulley with isopropyl alcohol; fit new Gates HTD3M belt; set tension
Inspect wheel encoder magnets: verify each magnet is flush and centered over Hall sensor using 0.1mm feeler gauge; re-epoxy any displaced magnets with Loctite EA 9466
Reassemble belly panel; power on; run wheel speed calibration from fleet console; confirm all 6 wheels tracking within ±2% of commanded speed

parts.part_name: Gates HTD3M Drive Belt 255-3M-9 (replacement set of 6)

failure_modes.symptom: stereo depth estimation errors causing collision avoidance false stops, one or more camera streams dropping from perception pipeline
failure_modes.root_cause: camera module ribbon cable fatigue fracture at body flex-point after repeated opening/closing of robot lid; secondary: OV2740 or similar CMOS sensor ESD damage from static discharge during courier interactions

repair_protocol.title: Camera Module Ribbon Cable Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: From fleet console, identify which camera streams are down (camera ID in perception log); locate physical position (front-left, rear-right, etc.)
Open robot lid fully; locate affected camera module (labeled on housing); wear ESD wrist strap before touching any electronics
steps.warning: CMOS sensors are ESD-sensitive — always ground yourself before handling

steps.action: Disconnect ribbon cable ZIF connector: lift retaining arm with plastic spudger; slide ribbon out; inspect for creasing, tears, or trace separation
If cable damaged: measure length and pitch (typically 15-24 pin, 0.5mm pitch FFC); source replacement; insert new cable fully before locking ZIF arm
Power on; confirm camera stream active in fleet console; run stereo calibration verification tool — reprojection error must be <0.5 pixels

failure_modes.symptom: range degradation >25%, BMS thermal warning at end of charge, pack voltage sag under acceleration load
failure_modes.root_cause: cyclic lithium plating on anode at fast-charge rates in cold weather (<10°C); BMS charge algorithm not compensating for low-temperature derating; cumulative capacity fade from 600+ shallow cycles

repair_protocol.title: Battery State-of-Health Assessment and BMS Firmware Update

steps.action: Pull BMS state-of-health report via fleet console; record SOH%, cycle count, max cell temperature and delta-voltage over last 30 days
If SOH <80%: flag battery for replacement; notify depot. If SOH 80-90%: proceed with BMS parameter update
Update BMS firmware to latest release (adds low-temperature charge derating profile: reduces charge current to 0.3C below 10°C)
steps.tool: fleet OTA console or USB-C service port
steps.warning: Do not interrupt OTA during BMS flash — causes BMS lockout requiring depot recovery

steps.action: After firmware update, perform a full conditioning cycle: discharge to 10% SOC at 0.5C, then charge at 0.3C to 100%; log capacity
If capacity recovered to >85% nameplate, return to service. If not, pull pack and ship to battery recycler per local hazmat regulations
steps.warning: Li-ion pack disposal is regulated waste — do not discard in general trash

predictive_signals.signal_source: BMS SOH register via fleet telemetry
BMS voltage monitor during 2C discharge pulse

failure_modes.symptom: robot stops at false obstacles in indoor/covered spaces, high false-positive rate near glass or smooth walls, one or more sensors not responding
failure_modes.root_cause: ultrasonic transducer membrane contamination from sidewalk grit/mud reduces signal-to-noise ratio; glass surfaces produce specular reflection causing signal loss; membrane cracking from physical impact

repair_protocol.title: Ultrasonic Sensor Cleaning and Sensitivity Calibration

steps.action: From fleet console, run ultrasonic self-test; identify sensors with SNR <6dB or range error >±15cm at 50cm reference target
Clean each sensor membrane with dry compressed air at <30 PSI from 10cm distance; remove loose grit without wetting membrane
steps.warning: Do not use liquid cleaners on ultrasonic membranes — wetting causes temporary desensitization

steps.action: For persistent grit adhesion: use soft camel-hair brush to dislodge; verify membrane visually for cracks
If membrane cracked: replace sensor module (push-fit retaining ring, no solder); recalibrate array after replacement
steps.tool: spanner wrench for sensor retaining ring

steps.action: Run recalibration routine from fleet console — drive robot alongside reference wall at 40cm distance for 5 meters; system auto-calibrates sensor offsets

failure_modes.component: mechanical lid hinge and latch assembly
failure_modes.symptom: lid fails to latch after delivery, triggering 'lid open' safety fault; hinge shows binding; lid rattles during travel
failure_modes.root_cause: stainless steel hinge pins develop fretting corrosion from repeated open/close cycles in humid environments; plastic latch housing cracks from customer mishandling (forced closure)

repair_protocol.title: Lid Hinge Pin Lubrication and Latch Housing Replacement

steps.action: Open lid; inspect hinge pins for fretting corrosion (reddish-brown powder around pin holes); check plastic latch housing for visible cracks
Apply Krytox GPL 205 grease to hinge pin interface via syringe; work grease into joint by cycling lid 10 times
If plastic latch housing cracked: use spudger to release 4 snap clips; pull housing off; install new housing; confirm positive latch engagement at <15N force
Test lid latch 20 cycles; verify fleet console 'lid state' sensor reads CLOSED when latched and OPEN when released

failure_modes.symptom: robot becomes stuck in a loop navigating the same 5m section of sidewalk, fails to reroute around temporary obstacle, or unexpectedly enters manual-assist request mode
failure_modes.root_cause: HD map data staleness from construction or seasonal changes; edge-case failures in obstacle cost-map when multiple dynamic agents (pedestrians, cyclists) cluster; watchdog timeout in path planner causing node restart

repair_protocol.title: Navigation Stack Reset and Map Cache Refresh

steps.action: If robot is stuck: send 'manual assist' command from fleet console; operator guides robot 2m past blockage; re-engage autonomy
After return to depot: SSH into robot; pull navigation logs from `/var/log/nav/` for the affected run; send to engineering team
Clear stale map tile cache: `nav_cache_reset --tiles --radius 200m --location <GPS_coords>`
Pull latest map update from fleet server: `nav_map_sync --force`; verify sync completion with `nav_map_status`
Run robot on affected route segment in 'shadow mode' (autonomy active but remote operator monitoring live); confirm clean navigation for 3 consecutive runs before removing monitoring

platforms.name: Amazon Proteus
platforms.notes: Amazon's first fully autonomous warehouse AMR operating among human workers without safety cages. Uses custom SLAM navigation.

failure_modes.component: omnidirectional drive module (mecanum / caster wheel assembly)
failure_modes.symptom: robot moves in unexpected direction, reported heading error >15 degrees from commanded, vibration on smooth warehouse floor
failure_modes.root_cause: mecanum wheel roller bearings develop flat spots from sustained high-load static positions during warehouse idle periods; polyurethane roller tread cracking from thermal cycling and solvent contact

repair_protocol.title: Mecanum Wheel Roller Bearing and Tread Inspection

steps.action: Bring robot to maintenance bay; command robot to home position via fleet console; engage lockout
Lift robot using floor jack under designated lift points (4x marked pads on chassis); support on jack stands
steps.warning: Proteus weighs 100kg unloaded — use rated jack stands rated for 250kg minimum

steps.action: Inspect each mecanum wheel (4 wheels, 8 rollers each): spin rollers by hand; check for flat spots (>1mm depth), cracking, or delamination of PU tread
For damaged rollers: remove roller axle bolt (M6); pull roller; inspect roller bearing (6000-2RS, 10x26x8mm); replace bearing and/or full roller assembly
steps.tool: 6mm hex key, bearing puller, SKF 6000-2RS bearing

steps.action: Replace PU tread if cracked: press off old tread; apply new PU roller; cure in oven per supplier datasheet (80°C, 2h)
Lower robot; run holonomic drive calibration from fleet console — strafe test left/right/diagonal; confirm position error <5mm over 3m

parts.part_name: Mecanum Wheel Polyurethane Roller (replacement set of 8)

failure_modes.symptom: robot fails to dock after 3 attempts, fleet shows 'dock failure' alarm, robot idles with low battery
failure_modes.root_cause: dock infrared guide beacon contamination from warehouse dust causing alignment signal loss >1cm; charging pad contact spring fatigue reducing contact pressure below spec; dock sensor drift from temperature expansion

repair_protocol.title: Docking Station Alignment Calibration and Contact Cleaning

steps.action: Lock out docking station from fleet console; confirm no robot attempting to dock; switch station to maintenance mode
steps.warning: Station carries 48V DC on charging contacts — always lockout before touching contacts

steps.action: Clean IR guide beacons (4 units on dock face) with isopropyl alcohol and cotton swab; clean robot-side IR receivers
Inspect charging pad spring contacts (2x brass contacts): measure spring force with force gauge — must be 2.0-3.5 N per contact; replace spring if <1.5 N
steps.tool: force gauge 0-5N, contact spring replacement tool

steps.action: Clean contact surfaces with 600-grit emery cloth to remove oxidation; wipe with isopropyl alcohol
Run dock alignment calibration from fleet console: robot approaches dock 5 times; system measures approach vector and auto-corrects dock beacon offset parameters
Confirm 5 consecutive successful docks; monitor charge current — must reach C/3 (1.6A for 4800Wh pack) within 30 seconds of dock contact

failure_modes.symptom: battery SOC reading jumps unexpectedly, thermal protection trip during charge cycle, robot returned to dock with 15% shown SOC but actually depleted
failure_modes.root_cause: LFP cell SOC estimation inaccuracy from BMS Coulomb counter drift — common in LFP chemistry due to flat discharge curve; thermal management fan bearing failure causing elevated cell temperatures; cell group parallel fusing degradation

repair_protocol.title: BMS Coulomb Counter Recalibration and Thermal Fan Service

steps.action: Pull full BMS data dump via fleet console: SOH%, cycle count, Coulomb counter accumulated error, individual cell voltages and temperatures
Run forced full calibration cycle: discharge to 2.5V/cell cutoff at C/5 rate; rest 2 hours; charge to 3.65V/cell at C/5; rest 2 hours; this resets Coulomb counter baseline
steps.warning: This cycle takes ~20 hours — schedule during off-shift maintenance window

steps.action: Update BMS firmware if version <3.2.1 (adds improved LFP SOC estimation using OCV table correction)
steps.tool: fleet OTA or service laptop USB-C

steps.action: Remove battery access panel; locate BMS cooling fan; spin by hand — must rotate freely; measure fan RPM at full speed with tachometer — must be >4500 RPM
steps.warning: Insulated gloves required — pack is 48V

steps.action: If fan RPM <4000 or grinding: replace fan (90mm 24VDC axial fan); apply fresh thermal paste to BMS controller heatsink
steps.tool: fan replacement, Arctic MX-4 thermal paste

steps.action: Confirm SOC accuracy over 2-week monitoring period; flag any SOC jump >5% between consecutive 10-minute telemetry readings

failure_modes.component: floor safety sensor array (3D ToF / laser scanner)
failure_modes.symptom: robot incorrectly detects floor hazard and stops; robot misses actual low obstacle (pallet edge, wire guide); E-stop triggers in clean aisle
failure_modes.root_cause: warehouse floor dust accumulation on downward-facing ToF sensor window; forklift pallet dust plumes create momentary high-particulate environment exceeding sensor noise threshold; lens scratches from floor surface proximity

repair_protocol.title: Floor Safety Sensor Cleaning and Threshold Recalibration

steps.action: Pull sensor false-positive log from fleet console for the past 7 days; identify sensors with >5 unconfirmed E-stop events
Engage robot lockout; lower robot to ground-level maintenance position; access floor sensor array (belly panel, 6x M4 screws)
Clean each ToF sensor window: blow off loose dust with dry compressed air; wipe with Zeiss Lens Care solution on microfibre cloth
steps.tool: compressed air, microfibre cloth, Zeiss Lens Care

steps.action: Inspect sensor windows under magnification for scratches >0.5mm; replace window cap if scratched (press-fit cap, no solder)
Reassemble; run floor sensor calibration routine from fleet console on clean flat floor — confirm all sensor range readings within ±10mm of 200mm reference
steps.tool: fleet console calibration, 200mm height reference block

steps.action: Update dust-filter threshold parameter in sensor config if warehouse has elevated particulate season (increase dust-hold-off window from 50ms to 100ms)
steps.warning: Increasing hold-off reduces safety response speed — safety engineer sign-off required

failure_modes.symptom: robot reports position jump >1m from expected location, fails to navigate to correct pick station, collides with rack corner
failure_modes.root_cause: feature-sparse warehouse environments (blank white walls) causing particle filter degeneration in SLAM; reflective floor causing LiDAR ground-return multipath; map invalidation when warehouse racks are reconfigured

repair_protocol.title: SLAM Map Rebuild and Localization Parameter Tuning

steps.action: Immediately halt robot from fleet console if position jump detected; engage E-stop; do not attempt autonomous navigation until map is verified
steps.warning: Robot operating with bad localization poses collision risk — treat as safety incident

steps.action: SSH into robot; pull SLAM log from `/var/log/slam/` for past 30 minutes; check particle weight distribution — if effective sample size <50, filter has degenerated
If map is stale (rack reconfiguration): run guided re-mapping: robot driven manually around full warehouse perimeter at 0.3 m/s; system rebuilds occupancy grid
Add artificial landmarks (retroreflective AprilTag markers) to feature-sparse walls if SLAM degeneration is recurring — mount at 1.5m height, 5m intervals
Tune SLAM parameters: increase lidar scan-match weight on corner features; reduce trust in dead-reckoning after 500ms; update config and restart SLAM node
Test robot over 30-minute autonomous run; confirm maximum localization error <50mm per fleet position tracking overlay

failure_modes.component: payload lift mechanism (scissor lift / conveyor interface)
failure_modes.symptom: robot cannot raise or lower payload to correct transfer height, lift mechanism grinds, fleet shows 'transfer failed' error
failure_modes.root_cause: lead screw drive nut wear from particulate contamination reducing lubrication film; limit switch failure from vibration causing incorrect height reporting; linear guide rail binding from improper loading

repair_protocol.title: Lift Mechanism Lead Screw Lubrication and Limit Switch Inspection

steps.action: Command lift to mid-travel position from fleet console; engage lockout; access lift mechanism via top service panel
steps.tool: fleet console, 4mm hex key for panel
steps.warning: Lift mechanism under spring preload — secure with locking pin before servicing

steps.action: Inspect lead screw: clean all grit from thread with stiff nylon brush; apply fresh Molykote 33 Light grease to full thread length
steps.tool: nylon brush, Molykote 33 Light grease, grease gun

steps.action: Inspect lead screw nut for thread wear: insert go/no-go thread gauge; replace nut if backlash >0.5mm
Inspect upper and lower limit switches: manually actuate; confirm click and electrical continuity; replace any switch that does not click cleanly
Run lift calibration from fleet console: 3 full up/down cycles; verify height at transfer point within ±3mm of specification
steps.tool: fleet console lift calibration, height reference block

predictive_signals.signal_source: lift encoder vs limit switch cross-check

platforms.name: Lime Gen4 E-Scooter
platforms.notes: Fleet e-scooter with IP56 rating. Gen4 introduced improved battery and IoT lock vs Gen3.

failure_modes.symptom: audible grinding or clicking from rear wheel during riding, vibration felt through deck, motor current draw elevated at constant speed
failure_modes.root_cause: 6002-2RS or similar bearing inner race spalling from contaminated grease after water ingress through worn axle seal; accelerated by high-speed riding over rough pavement transmitting impact loads to bearing race

repair_protocol.title: Hub Motor Bearing Replacement — Lime Gen4

steps.action: Release scooter from service lock via fleet console; disable IoT lock; bring to workshop
Remove axle retention nuts (M10, 32mm AF); slide rear wheel off dropouts; disconnect motor phase wires and hall sensor connector
steps.warning: Label motor phase wires before disconnection — wrong reconnection causes reverse motor direction

steps.action: Use hub motor disassembly tool to remove motor side covers; extract rotor from stator; place on clean bench
steps.tool: hub motor disassembly tool set, bench vise

steps.action: Press out bearing from rotor carrier on drive side; inspect bearing race and balls under 10x magnification for spalling, pitting, or brinelling
Clean bearing bore with isopropyl alcohol; apply thin film Loctite 648; press new NTN 6002-2RS bearing; allow 20-minute cure at room temperature
Pack inner bearing cavity with Lucas Red N Tacky EP2 grease (5g per bearing cavity); reassemble motor; torque side cover bolts to 8 Nm
Reinstall wheel; connect motor wires (verify phase order with fleet console motor test); torque axle nuts to 35 Nm
Run 10-minute ride cycle on roller dyno or flat surface; verify motor current within ±10% baseline; verify no audible noise
steps.tool: roller dyno or flat test area, fleet console

parts.part_name: Lucas Red N Tacky EP2 Grease 1lb

failure_modes.component: battery BMS and cell pack (Li-ion NMC, 504Wh)
failure_modes.symptom: scooter range drops to <30km before recharge warning, cells fail to balance at end of charge cycle, BMS reports cell group 3 voltage 200mV below others
failure_modes.root_cause: NMC cell capacity fade from repeated partial charging during fleet rebalancing operations; cell imbalance from 1-2 cells in the parallel group developing higher internal resistance from micro-short nucleation; BMS passive balancing undersized for fast fleet cycling

repair_protocol.title: Battery Pack Balance Assessment and Cell Group Replacement

steps.action: Pull BMS telemetry from fleet cloud dashboard; review 30-day cell voltage trends; identify persistent low-voltage cell groups
Remove battery pack: unlock battery door (Torx T25, 3 screws); slide out pack; disconnect Anderson SB50 connector
steps.tool: Torx T25 screwdriver, Anderson disconnect tool
steps.warning: 48V pack — insulated gloves mandatory; avoid shorting Anderson connector terminals

steps.action: Connect pack to TI BQ78350 or Victron Energy BMV-712 battery monitor via balance connector; take individual cell group voltage measurements
steps.tool: TI BQ78350 eval kit or Victron BMV-712, multimeter

steps.action: Identify any cell group showing voltage delta >100mV after 2-hour passive balance rest; flag those cell groups for replacement
If <3 cell groups affected: replace individual cell modules (spot-weld nickel strip tabs); test weld strength by trying to peel at 45 degrees — must not lift at <20N
steps.tool: battery spot welder, nickel strip 0.15mm, PPE
steps.warning: Cell replacement requires battery technician certification — high short-circuit energy

steps.action: If >3 cell groups affected: replace full pack; recycle old pack per local hazmat regulations (IATA/EPA)
Reinstall pack; run 3 full conditioning cycles; confirm range >45km before returning to fleet

predictive_signals.signal_source: BMS cell monitor via fleet cloud

failure_modes.component: brake cable and mechanical disc brake caliper
failure_modes.symptom: brake lever feels spongy, longer stopping distance, Segway/Lime safety audit flags brake performance below spec; brake rub noise at low speed
failure_modes.root_cause: stainless brake cable stretch and outer housing compression after 300-500 hours of use; brake pad wear reducing effective pad thickness to <2mm; caliper piston contamination from road spray

repair_protocol.title: Brake Cable Adjustment, Pad Inspection, and Caliper Service

steps.action: Perform brake performance check: measure stopping distance from 20 km/h on dry concrete — must be <7m; if >7m, remove from service immediately
steps.tool: GPS speed checker or fleet speed log, tape measure
steps.warning: Do not return scooter to service with stopping distance >7m — safety-critical

steps.action: Adjust brake cable at handlebar barrel adjuster: turn adjuster counter-clockwise until lever has 2-3mm of free play before engagement
If adjustment range exhausted: replace brake cable — thread new cable through housing; set barrel adjuster to middle of range; pull cable taut at caliper anchor; pinch bolt to 6 Nm
steps.tool: cable cutters, 4mm hex key, torque screwdriver

steps.action: Inspect brake pads: remove caliper inspection plug; measure pad thickness with depth gauge — must be >2.5mm; replace pads if <2.5mm
Clean caliper piston with brake cleaner spray; actuate caliper by hand to confirm piston moves freely; apply caliper piston grease to piston seal if stiff
steps.tool: brake cleaner spray, caliper piston tool, Sil-Glyde caliper grease
steps.warning: Never use petroleum-based lubricant on brake pads or rotor — use only silicone-based caliper grease on piston seal

steps.action: Repeat stopping distance test; confirm <7m from 20 km/h; log result in fleet maintenance record
steps.tool: tape measure, fleet console maintenance log

predictive_signals.signal_source: brake lever force sensor (if equipped) or proxy from brake engagement angle

failure_modes.symptom: visible crack or fracture in deck platform, flex or creak when rider applies weight, surface delamination of non-slip mat
failure_modes.root_cause: fatigue crack nucleation at deck-to-stem weld joint from repeated impact loading (curb drops, potholes); overloading by riders exceeding 100kg weight limit; material fatigue in 6061-T6 aluminum at weld HAZ

repair_protocol.title: Deck Structural Inspection and Retirement Decision

steps.action: Perform visual inspection of full deck surface and underside; check stem-to-deck weld joint with 10x loupe for any crack indication
steps.warning: Any confirmed crack means immediate retirement — do not attempt to weld repair deck in field

steps.action: Apply penetrant dye test to suspect areas: spray red dye; wait 5 minutes; clean; spray developer; pink indication = crack present
steps.tool: Magnaflux Spotcheck SK-816 dye penetrant kit

steps.action: If crack confirmed: remove scooter from service permanently; log in fleet system as 'retired structural failure'; send for recycling
steps.warning: Do not re-deploy a cracked deck scooter — liability risk for catastrophic rider injury

steps.action: If no crack but non-slip mat delaminating: remove old mat with heat gun and plastic scraper; clean deck with acetone; apply new 3M Safety-Walk 600 Series non-slip tape
steps.tool: heat gun, plastic scraper, acetone, roller

parts.part_name: Magnaflux Spotcheck SK-816 Dye Penetrant Kit

failure_modes.component: IoT lock module (NFC + Bluetooth + cellular unlock)
failure_modes.symptom: scooter cannot be unlocked by rider app, stuck in locked state in field; fleet shows scooter as available but unlock command times out
failure_modes.root_cause: IoT lock MCU firmware hang from malformed BLE advertisement packet; cellular modem SIM card session expiry not handled gracefully; water ingress via cracked lock housing O-ring corroding PCB traces

repair_protocol.title: IoT Lock Module Reset, Firmware Update, and Housing Inspection

steps.action: Attempt remote power cycle of IoT module via fleet console (OTA reset command); wait 90 seconds; retry unlock command
If OTA reset fails: physically retrieve scooter with maintenance key (mechanical override key); transport to workshop
In workshop: access IoT module (2x Torx T10 screws on lock housing); inspect PCB for water damage (green/white corrosion deposits)
If corrosion present: clean PCB with 99% isopropyl alcohol and soft brush; re-bake in oven at 60°C for 30 minutes to drive off moisture
steps.warning: Do not exceed 70°C — will damage BLE antenna matching circuit components

steps.action: Update IoT firmware via USB-C service port to latest release (adds BLE watchdog timeout and SIM session auto-renew)
steps.tool: USB-C service cable, laptop with Lime fleet firmware tool

steps.action: Inspect housing O-ring; replace if compressed, cracked, or oval-deformed; apply thin film of Molykote 111 to new O-ring before installation
Reassemble; test unlock via rider app and fleet console; confirm <3 second unlock response time over LTE

parts.part_name: IoT Lock Housing O-Ring Replacement Set

failure_modes.component: front fork (aluminum steering column / fork legs)
failure_modes.symptom: loose or notchy headset bearing, steering wobble at speed, audible clunk when braking, visible fork leg crack at dropout
failure_modes.root_cause: headset bearing preload loss from vibration loosening lock nut; fork leg fatigue cracking at dropout weld from high-cycle impact loading; corrosion fatigue at anodized-to-raw aluminum interface at welds

repair_protocol.title: Headset Bearing Adjustment and Fork Structural Inspection

steps.action: Apply front brake; rock scooter forward and back — any clunk or movement indicates headset play; lift front wheel and turn bars — any binding indicates headset overtightening
Adjust headset bearing: loosen stem clamp bolts; adjust top cap bolt in 1/4-turn increments until play eliminated without binding; tighten stem clamp bolts to 6 Nm
Inspect both fork legs at dropout (axle retention slot): apply dye penetrant test; any crack indication = remove from service permanently
steps.warning: Fork crack is a retire-immediately defect — catastrophic failure risk at speed

steps.action: Inspect fork crown weld (joint between fork legs and steerer tube): look for hairline cracks or paint bubbling over welds with 10x loupe
If headset bearing races pitted or gritty: remove stem; remove fork; press out bearing races; install new 1-1/8 inch integrated headset bearing; repack with Shimano grease
steps.tool: headset press, bearing cup remover, Shimano grease

parts.part_name: FSA Orbit IS-2 1-1/8 Integrated Headset Bearing

platforms.name: RadCommercial Cargo E-Bike
platforms.notes: Commercial cargo e-bike rated IP67. Designed for last-mile delivery fleets. 750W hub motor, front suspension fork.

failure_modes.component: 750W rear hub motor — BLDC windings and bearings
failure_modes.symptom: motor cuts out above 80% throttle, error code 21 on display (over-current), motor housing hot to touch after 20-minute ride, audible bearing grind
failure_modes.root_cause: winding insulation breakdown from repeated thermal cycling with 113kg payload on hills; concurrent bearing race spalling from high-load radial forces under cargo weight; inadequate motor cooling in IP67 sealed housing

repair_protocol.title: 750W Hub Motor Winding Inspection and Bearing Replacement

steps.action: Remove rear wheel: shift to smallest cog; disconnect torque arm (2x M5 bolts); unplug motor connector (3+5 pin); remove axle nuts (15mm AF); extract wheel

Remove hub motor cover (6x M4 bolts, Torx T20); extract rotor; inspect stator windings under inspection light — any blackening, smell of burnt varnish, or winding-to-winding short = full motor replacement
Measure winding resistance (phase-to-phase) with multimeter: must be within ±5% of spec (typically 0.2-0.5 Ω per phase for 750W). Any phase reading open circuit or >20% deviation = replace motor
If windings OK: press out drive-side bearing (6203-2RS); inspect non-drive side bearing; replace both
Install new NTN 6203-2RS bearings with Loctite 648 in bore; pack bearing cavities with 3-4g Mobil Polyrex EM grease
Reinstall motor cover with new IP67 gasket; torque bolts in star pattern to 8 Nm
Reinstall wheel; reconnect motor connector (verify phase order if connector replaced); run 15-minute loaded test with 80kg rider+cargo; check for error codes and motor temp <70°C
steps.tool: fleet console or display, IR thermometer

parts.part_name: Mobil Polyrex EM Electric Motor Grease 1lb

failure_modes.component: hydraulic disc brake system (front and rear)
failure_modes.symptom: brake lever goes to bar without full stopping force, brake fade on long descents, brake fluid reservoir low indicator, squealing from rotor
failure_modes.root_cause: hydraulic brake fluid (DOT 4 or mineral oil depending on caliper brand) moisture absorption degrading boiling point over 12-18 months; pad material glazing from repeated light braking with heavy cargo; rotor surface oxidation from IP67-sealed environments

repair_protocol.title: Hydraulic Brake Fluid Replacement and Caliper Bleed

steps.action: Identify brake fluid type from caliper manufacturer (Tektro, Shimano = mineral oil; SRAM, Magura DOT 5.1 — never mix DOT with mineral)
steps.warning: Mixing DOT and mineral oil destroys caliper seals — catastrophic brake failure

steps.action: Position bike upright; remove brake lever reservoir cap; remove old fluid with syringe; clean reservoir with fresh fluid of same type
Attach bleed kit to caliper bleed nipple; open nipple 1/4 turn; slowly push fresh fluid from lever reservoir through system until clean fluid exits caliper bleed port
steps.tool: Shimano TL-BT03-S or Tektro bleed kit, fresh mineral oil

steps.action: Close bleed nipple; top up reservoir to max line; reinstall cap; check lever firmness — must not bottom out before full braking engagement
Inspect pad thickness: if <1.5mm compound remaining, replace pads; bed in new pads per manufacturer procedure (10x stops from 30kmh to 5kmh, allow cool between sets)
Sand rotor surface with 400-grit wet/dry sandpaper to deglaze; wipe with isopropyl alcohol; test brake performance — stopping distance from 25km/h must be <9m
steps.tool: 400-grit wet/dry paper, isopropyl alcohol, tape measure

parts.part_name: Tektro Hydraulic Disc Brake Pad Set E10.11

predictive_signals.signal_source: brake pressure sensor (if equipped) or proxy rider report

failure_modes.symptom: range below 35km on flat route, battery LED shows 3 bars but bike cuts power after 15 minutes, charge port intermittent connection
failure_modes.root_cause: cell capacity fade from repeated full-depth discharge in commercial delivery use; charge port connector oxidation from condensation inside IP67 enclosure via thermal cycling; BMS over-discharge protection triggering prematurely due to cell imbalance

repair_protocol.title: Battery Charge Port Cleaning and BMS Balance Cycle

steps.action: Inspect charge port with magnification loupe: look for corrosion, bent pins, or debris in port; clean with DeoxIT D5 on cotton swab
steps.tool: magnification loupe, DeoxIT D5, cotton swab
steps.warning: Ensure bike is powered off before cleaning charge port

steps.action: Plug in charger: confirm LED shows charging (red/amber) within 10 seconds; if no indication, test charger output voltage with multimeter (should be 58.8V for 48V system)
If charger output correct but bike not charging: access battery BMS via diagnostic port (if equipped); run BMS reset command; attempt charge again
Run BMS balance charge: set charger to 100% SOC; allow full absorb phase (current drops to C/20 or lower) — this forces passive balancing; may take 6-8 hours
After balance charge: fully discharge at controlled C/3 rate (load tester or ride); re-charge fully; measure range on standard flat route — if <40km, schedule pack replacement

predictive_signals.signal_source: GPS track distance / charge cycle log
charger current profile ramp time proxy

failure_modes.symptom: gear skipping under load, chain dropping under heavy cargo pedaling, derailleur bent from impact, indexed shifting out of alignment after 500km
failure_modes.root_cause: chain elongation from 500+ hours of high-torque cargo use causing skip on cassette cogs; derailleur hanger bending from curb/rack impacts; shift cable stretch de-indexing Shimano 7-speed precision

repair_protocol.title: Chain Wear Check, Derailleur Alignment, and Cable Adjustment

steps.action: Measure chain with Park Tool CC-4 chain checker — if >0.75% elongation reading, replace chain; if >1.0%, inspect cassette for hooked cog teeth (requires cassette replacement too)
steps.tool: Park Tool CC-4 chain wear indicator
steps.warning: Running a worn chain destroys cassette cogs — replace chain at 0.75% to avoid cascade cost

steps.action: Replace chain: break old chain with chain breaker; thread new KMC Z7 chain; connect with master link; ensure correct direction (inner-plate printing faces outward)
steps.tool: Park Tool CT-3.2 chain breaker, KMC Missing Link pliers

steps.action: Inspect derailleur hanger: place derailleur alignment gauge in axle hole; rotate gauge around wheel — any deviation >2mm at rim = straighten or replace hanger
steps.warning: Only straighten hanger if aluminum — carbon hangers must be replaced, not bent

steps.action: Adjust shift cable tension at barrel adjuster until index shifting is clean through all 7 gears under light pedaling load
Set derailleur limit screws (H and L): confirm chain does not overshoot onto spokes or into dropout in any gear
Test under cargo load (40kg on rear rack); shift through all gears under moderate torque; confirm no skipping for 3 consecutive lap cycles

predictive_signals.signal_source: rider report or motor current spike analysis

failure_modes.symptom: fork bottoms out on speed bumps with full cargo, oil stain on fork legs, fork feels harsh or locked at one end of travel
failure_modes.root_cause: fork oil seal degradation from UV exposure and grit ingress causing oil bypass; spring preload inadequate for 113kg cargo payload at maximum; damper shim stack fatigue after 800+ hours of cargo-loaded riding

repair_protocol.title: Suspension Fork Seal Replacement and Oil Change

steps.action: Place bike in stand; remove front wheel; support fork with one hand; loosen axle through-bolt (5mm hex)
Remove fork lower legs: unscrew lower leg bolts (4mm hex, bottom of each leg); pull lowers down firmly while twisting to break seal; drain old oil into container
steps.warning: Old fork oil is hazardous — dispose per local regulations, not in drain

steps.action: Inspect dust seals and oil seals: replace with correct seal kit (matched to fork model — typically 32mm or 34mm stanchion diameter)
steps.tool: seal driver or large socket, wooden dowel

steps.action: Measure and add correct volume of suspension fork oil: 10wt oil, quantity per fork manufacturer spec (typically 80-120ml per leg); reassemble lower legs; torque bottom bolts to 8 Nm
steps.tool: graduated syringe, 10wt suspension oil, torque wrench

steps.action: Reinstall wheel; set preload to maximum for cargo use (full clockwise on preload adjuster); test with 40kg cargo load — fork should use 70-80% travel on firm bump

parts.part_name: RST / SR Suntour 32mm Fork Seal Kit

parts.supplier: Fox Racing Shox / Chain Reaction Cycles

predictive_signals.signal_source: visual inspection flag in fleet maintenance log

platforms.name: Figure 02
platforms.notes: Commercial humanoid with OpenAI VLA integration. BMW manufacturing deployment. Documentation is sparse — failure modes based on generic humanoid engineering principles. Confidence: low.

failure_modes.component: leg actuator (knee / hip joint — harmonic drive + BLDC)
failure_modes.symptom: joint position error alarms, gait asymmetry detected by IMU, joint makes clicking sound during stance phase, reduced torque output in affected limb
failure_modes.root_cause: harmonic drive flex spline fatigue cracking from repetitive high-torque stance loading; wave generator bearing wear increasing backlash beyond firmware compensation range; motor winding thermal stress from sustained walking duty cycle

repair_protocol.title: Leg Actuator Joint Inspection and Harmonic Drive Assessment

steps.action: Command robot to maintenance pose via control interface (lying flat, all joints to home position); engage full software E-stop and lockout
steps.warning: Humanoid joints store significant energy in springs/actuators — never manually move limbs without confirming software lockout

steps.action: Measure joint backlash with dial indicator on output link: apply ±5 Nm hand torque; read dial indicator deflection at 200mm moment arm — backlash >0.3mm indicates harmonic drive wear
steps.tool: dial indicator, 200mm moment arm fixture, force gauge

steps.action: Pull joint torque telemetry from last 7 days: plot torque vs position — increasing torque-to-position ratio (stiffness increase) or torque ripple >15% indicates flex spline damage
If backlash >0.3mm or torque ripple >15%: remove actuator module (typically 6-8 M5 bolts per actuator, 2 motor connectors); send to manufacturer for harmonic drive replacement — not field-repairable
steps.tool: M5 hex key set, torque wrench, connector extraction tool
steps.warning: Harmonic drive replacement requires precision assembly tooling and calibration — depot/manufacturer service only

steps.action: After actuator reinstallation: run full joint calibration routine from robot software; verify joint position accuracy <0.1° across full range of motion

parts.part_name: Harmonic Drive Component Set (wave generator + flex spline + circular spline)
BLDC Actuator Replacement Module (Figure AI OEM)

failure_modes.symptom: robot stumbles on uneven terrain it previously handled, balance controller intervention rate increases, 'sensor fusion degraded' warning in logs
failure_modes.root_cause: IMU calibration drift from thermal cycling and vibration causing accelerometer bias error accumulation; camera-to-IMU extrinsic calibration drift from vibration loosening camera mount; temporal synchronization error between sensor streams (>5ms) causing state estimation noise

repair_protocol.title: IMU Calibration Reset and Camera Extrinsic Recalibration

steps.action: Place robot on precision flat surface (±0.1° level); run IMU static calibration routine from control software: robot must be stationary for 5 minutes; software estimates gyro bias and accelerometer offset
steps.warning: Robot must be on vibration-isolated surface for calibration — do not run on concrete near forklifts or HVAC equipment

steps.action: After IMU calibration, run camera-IMU temporal synchronization check: wave calibration target in front of cameras; software estimates time offset between camera frames and IMU samples — must be <2ms
steps.tool: AprilTag calibration target, robot calibration software

steps.action: Run camera extrinsic calibration: mount multi-tag calibration board at known distance; drive robot through slow head movements; software updates camera-to-IMU rigid body transform
steps.tool: multi-tag calibration board (0.5m x 0.5m), robot software

steps.action: Verify calibration: run robot on standard test course (flat, 10cm step, 5° ramp, 180° turn); balance intervention rate should be <5 per minute
steps.tool: standard test course, robot telemetry logger

steps.action: If calibration does not converge (residual error >0.5°): inspect camera mount bolts for looseness (M2.5 or M3); retorque; repeat calibration

parts.part_name: AprilTag Multi-Tag Calibration Board A2 size

failure_modes.symptom: battery temperature warning above 40°C during operation, charging stops at 80% SOC as thermal protection engages, reduced runtime from 5h to 3h
failure_modes.root_cause: cooling loop pump flow reduction from air bubble accumulation in liquid cooling circuit; thermal interface pad dry-out between battery cells and cooling plate after 18 months; cell chemistry sensitivity to BMW factory floor ambient temperature (28-35°C)

repair_protocol.title: Battery Cooling Circuit Bleed and Thermal Pad Inspection

steps.action: Pull battery thermal telemetry for past 14 days: plot peak cell temperature vs ambient; if delta >12°C at rest, suspect cooling degradation

Access battery cooling loop service port (location varies — refer to Figure AI service bulletin); attach cooling system pressure gauge; measure static pressure — must hold 0.8 bar over 10 minutes (no drop = no leak)
steps.tool: cooling system pressure gauge, Figure AI service docs

steps.action: Bleed cooling loop: open bleed nipple at highest point in loop; run pump at low speed; wait for continuous coolant flow without bubbles from bleed nipple; close nipple
If temperature delta persists after bleed: access battery cooling plate (specialist-level disassembly); replace Bergquist GP2000 thermal pads between cells and cooling plate
steps.tool: Bergquist GP2000 thermal pad, precision knife, isopropyl alcohol
steps.warning: Battery disassembly at this level is manufacturer service only — high voltage exposure

steps.action: After service: run full charge/discharge cycle in 25°C ambient; confirm peak cell temp <38°C throughout

predictive_signals.signal_source: battery NTC array via BMS telemetry

failure_modes.component: dexterous hand actuators (Figure AI proprietary — 16 DOF)
failure_modes.symptom: finger position error exceeds ±2mm during grasp tasks, object drop rate increases, tendon/cable pops audibly during gripping cycle
failure_modes.root_cause: tendon cable stretch and conduit wear in distal finger segments from repetitive pick-and-place with hard objects; pulley bearing micro-pitting in small-diameter (3-5mm) stainless bearings; tendon anchor crimp fatigue failure

repair_protocol.title: Hand Tendon Cable Inspection and Tension Calibration

steps.action: Command hand to open/close calibration cycle 10 times via control software; record finger position error from encoder readings — identify fingers with error >1.5mm
Engage hand lockout; open hand dorsal access panel (snap-fit cover, plastic pry tool); expose tendon routing and pulley assembly
steps.tool: plastic pry tool, ESD wrist strap
steps.warning: Small bearings and springs — work over clean white surface to catch dropped components

steps.action: For each affected finger: pull tendon gently and feel for slack >0.5mm; inspect cable sheath for fraying under magnification; check anchor crimp for any wire escape
If tendon slack >1mm: adjust tension at motor-side tensioner (typically small M1.6 adjuster bolt) until slack is <0.3mm under 2N pull force
steps.warning: Over-tensioning tears finger tendons — apply maximum 2N at finger tip

steps.action: Replace any cable showing >3 broken strands or anchor crimp damage; route new cable per manufacturer routing diagram; crimp new anchor with ratchet crimper to 50N pull-out force
steps.tool: ratchet crimper, replacement cable, routing diagram
steps.warning: Cable routing errors can jam finger mechanism — follow diagram exactly

steps.action: Reassemble; run finger calibration routine; confirm all fingers within ±1mm position error over 20-cycle test

failure_modes.symptom: robot executes correct high-level task command but physical motion is jerky or mis-timed, model inference latency spikes >200ms, robot requests human clarification for tasks it previously completed autonomously
failure_modes.root_cause: VLA model version mismatch between edge compute and cloud after OTA partial update; real-time controller firmware clock synchronization drift from IEEE 1588 PTP network issue; model quantization artifacts at runtime causing action prediction error

repair_protocol.title: Firmware Version Verification and VLA Model Redeployment

steps.action: From fleet management console: check firmware version on all robot compute modules vs current release manifest; identify any module running non-current version
Check PTP synchronization status: `ptp4l -f /etc/ptp4l.conf --slave_only 2>&1 | grep offset` — offset must be <50 microseconds; if >500μs, restart PTP service
If VLA model mismatch: force full model re-sync: `vla_deploy --full-sync --verify`; confirm model hash matches fleet manifest before allowing autonomous operation
steps.tool: SSH terminal / fleet console OTA tool
steps.warning: Do not allow robot to operate autonomously with unverified VLA model — task execution may be unpredictable

steps.action: After model sync: run 10-task qualification test suite (pick, place, navigate, handoff); confirm task success rate >95% and motion profile latency <150ms
If motion jerkiness persists post-sync: reboot all real-time control modules in sequence (joint controllers → state estimator → planner); confirm boot sequence completes without fault codes

failure_modes.symptom: robot unable to detect ground contact reliably during foot-strike, stumbles on transitions between surface types, gait controller reports 'contact uncertainty' warnings
failure_modes.root_cause: foot force/torque sensor overload from hard foot-strikes on concrete exceeding sensor rated load (common in early-learning gait policies); cable damage to sensor signal wiring from flex fatigue at ankle pivot; sensor zero-offset drift from temperature

repair_protocol.title: Foot Force-Torque Sensor Zero Calibration and Cable Inspection

steps.action: Command robot to stand on flat surface, feet fully loaded; run force sensor zero calibration from software — sets current load as zero reference (tare)
steps.warning: Robot must be stationary and stable during tare — any motion will corrupt calibration

steps.action: Run sensor linearity check: apply known weights (5kg, 10kg, 15kg test masses) to foot pad; verify sensor output within ±3% of expected value
steps.tool: calibrated test masses, robot diagnostic dashboard

steps.action: If sensor overrange alarm has triggered: inspect sensor housing for mechanical damage; check for zero offset >5% of full-scale after tare — indicates overload damage requiring sensor replacement
Inspect ankle flex cable: bend cable through full ankle range while monitoring resistance continuity on each wire — any intermittent (>±5% change) = replace cable loom
After service: run gait validation on 10m test course with 5cm step transitions; confirm no 'contact uncertainty' warnings in 3 consecutive runs

parts.part_name: 6-Axis Force/Torque Sensor (generic humanoid ankle size ~50Nm range)

platforms.name: Tesla Optimus Gen2
platforms.notes: Tesla in-house manufacturing humanoid. Minimal external technical documentation. Failure modes based on engineering principles and publicly observable hardware. Confidence: low. FCC filings and teardown speculation are primary sources.

failure_modes.component: proprietary linear actuator (leg push-rod / knee extension)
failure_modes.symptom: joint stiffness increase, linear actuator audible buzz at near-max extension, gait deviation detected by Tesla's internal vision-based gait monitor, joint position error >1cm
failure_modes.root_cause: ball-screw drive nut preload loss from thermal cycling causing backlash increase; recirculating ball bearing wear from grit contamination in partially-sealed IP environment; motor winding insulation thermal degradation from high duty cycle in manufacturing environment (sustained walking)

repair_protocol.title: Linear Actuator Backlash Check and Ball-Screw Inspection

steps.action: Command Optimus to maintenance pose (supine, all joints centered); engage full E-stop via Tesla Optimus control tablet; verify all joint brakes engaged
steps.warning: Tesla actuators contain high-tension springs — do not manually manipulate limbs without confirmed brake engagement

steps.action: Measure linear actuator backlash: attach dial indicator to output rod end; apply ±20N axial hand load; record total indicator movement — must be <0.2mm
If backlash >0.3mm: remove actuator module (connector-first, then structural bolts — typically 4x M6); open actuator housing to expose ball-screw nut
steps.tool: M6 hex key, actuator housing tool
steps.warning: Module is under-documented externally — photograph all wiring before disconnection

steps.action: Inspect ball-screw balls and nut races under magnification: replace full ball-screw assembly if any pitting visible; clean housing of metal debris
Repack ball-screw nut with Mobil Mobilux EP2 grease (5-8g); reassemble; verify backlash <0.15mm before reinstallation
steps.tool: grease syringe, dial indicator, Mobilux EP2

steps.action: Reinstall actuator; run joint calibration from Tesla software; confirm position tracking error <0.5mm over 10 full-range motion cycles

parts.part_name: Thomson BSA12-3.175x3 Ball Screw Assembly (nearest commercial equivalent)
Mobil Mobilux EP2 Lithium Complex Grease 1kg

failure_modes.symptom: finger grip force inconsistency >20% cycle-to-cycle, tendon pops audible during rapid close gesture, finger tip position wobbles during precision tasks
failure_modes.root_cause: tendon cable micro-fatigue at sheave radius for small-bend (<8mm) pulleys common in 22-DOF hand architecture; actuator tendon pre-tensioner spring setpoint drift causing inconsistent tendon baseline tension; contact pad surface wear reducing tactile signal quality

repair_protocol.title: Hand Tendon Pre-Tension Inspection and Cable Integrity Check

steps.action: Run hand self-test cycle from Tesla control software: 20 open/close cycles at 50% speed; log finger position deviation; identify fingers with >3mm tip deviation
Engage hand lockout; open dorsal access cover (proprietary tool or Torx T6 if accessible); expose tendon routing and pulley tree
steps.tool: Tesla hand service tool or Torx T6
steps.warning: Hand contains small ABEC-7 bearings and springs — work on anti-static mat over white paper

steps.action: For each problematic finger: pull tendon at proximal segment with 1N force; measure slack — acceptable <0.5mm; if >1mm, adjust at pre-tensioner
Inspect each tendon under 10x loupe at pulley contact point: any fraying, kinking, or >2 broken strands = replace cable segment
Replace any failed tendon segment with 0.5mm 7x7 stainless aircraft cable; route per hand assembly diagram; crimp end with ratchet crimper; verify 30N pull-out from crimp
Reassemble; run 50-cycle qualification at full speed; confirm tip deviation <1.5mm and grip force consistency >90%
steps.tool: Tesla control software, grip force test object

tools_required: Tesla hand service tool or Torx T6

predictive_signals.signal_source: grip force sensor / actuator torque proxy

failure_modes.component: Tesla FSD SoC compute platform (onboard inference)
failure_modes.symptom: increased task latency, motion planning solution not found within 500ms timeout, SoC thermal throttle warning in logs, robot enters conservative 'reduced capability' mode
failure_modes.root_cause: FSD SoC operating at 90%+ CPU/NPU utilization from perception pipeline workload in cluttered manufacturing environment; thermal throttle from cooling system dust accumulation on heat sink fins; NPU SRAM soft errors from cosmic ray single-event upsets (rare but documented in ARM cores)

repair_protocol.title: FSD SoC Thermal Cleaning and Utilization Optimization

steps.action: Pull SoC performance telemetry: CPU utilization, NPU utilization, thermal zone temperatures, throttle events per hour over past 7 days
Access compute module cooling air inlet (location per Tesla service guide — typically rear torso); use compressed air at 30 PSI to clear dust from heat sink fins; measure airflow improvement
steps.tool: compressed air can 30 PSI, airflow indicator strip
steps.warning: ESD precaution — ground yourself before accessing compute compartment

steps.action: Check thermal interface between SoC and heat sink: if SoC package temp >85°C at 50% NPU load, replace thermal interface material; apply Shin-Etsu X-23 paste 0.1mm layer
steps.tool: Shin-Etsu X-23 thermal paste, plastic spatula, isopropyl alcohol

steps.action: If NPU SRAM errors in logs (ECC error count >0 per week): perform memory ECC scrub via Tesla diagnostic tool; if persistent, SoC replacement required (factory service)
Optimize perception pipeline configuration: reduce camera resolution for non-safety-critical views from 1080p to 720p in stable-lighting environments; confirm NPU utilization drops to <75%
steps.warning: Do not reduce resolution on safety-critical forward-facing cameras without engineering sign-off

failure_modes.component: rotary actuator (shoulder / hip — BLDC + cycloidal or planetary gearbox)
failure_modes.symptom: joint torque output drops to 60% of commanded, robot shifts to low-torque mode for affected limb, gear noise at low speed, joint vibration during slow controlled motion
failure_modes.root_cause: cycloidal gearbox pin roller bearing fatigue from high-cycle manufacturing use; planetary carrier bearing wear from sustained radial loads; motor encoder magnetic interference from adjacent actuators causing commutation errors

repair_protocol.title: Rotary Actuator Gearbox Inspection and Bearing Service

steps.action: Pull actuator efficiency telemetry: compare motor input power vs output torque × speed; efficiency <75% indicates gearbox loss; cross-check with gear noise audio analysis if equipped
Command robot to maintenance pose; lockout; remove actuator module: disconnect motor connector and encoder cable; remove 4-6x M6 structural bolts; extract module
steps.tool: M6 hex key, connector extraction tool, torque wrench

steps.action: On bench: measure gear stage backlash — apply ±10 Nm to output; measure input shaft rotation (should be <0.05° for cycloidal, <0.1° for planetary)
Open gearbox (proprietary fasteners expected): inspect pin rollers and ring gear; any pitting >0.1mm on pin rollers = replace cycloidal stage; record all gear measurements
Replace carrier bearings with matched precision set (ABEC-5 or better); repack with Klüber Isoflex NBU 15 ultra-precision bearing grease; reassemble gearbox to 0.02mm backlash spec
steps.tool: ABEC-5 bearing set, precision assembly fixtures, Klüber Isoflex NBU 15 grease
steps.warning: Cycloidal gearbox reassembly requires precision tooling — factory service only for full disassembly

steps.action: Reinstall actuator module; run efficiency test; confirm >85% mechanical efficiency; run position accuracy test <0.2° error at max rated torque

parts.part_name: Klüber Isoflex NBU 15 Ultra-Precision Bearing Grease 50g
ABEC-5 Precision Bearing Set (cycloidal carrier — OEM match)

predictive_signals.signal_source: motor power in vs joint torque × speed out

failure_modes.symptom: runtime reduced from 8h target to 5h, battery max charge voltage reduced by BMS to 90% capacity, coolant pump warning LED, pack surface temperature uneven
failure_modes.root_cause: liquid coolant micro-channel blockage from mineral precipitation in non-deionized coolant; cooling pump brushless motor bearing wear reducing flow rate; cell chemistry (assumed NMC 811 or LFP based on Tesla supply chain) sensitivity to >38°C cell temperature during fast charge

repair_protocol.title: Battery Cooling Pump Inspection and Coolant Flush

steps.action: Pull cooling system telemetry: inlet vs outlet temperature differential (ΔT); must be 5-8°C at nominal load; ΔT >12°C indicates low flow; ΔT <2°C indicates no flow or short-circuit
Access cooling system service port (Tesla Optimus — assumed similar to Tesla vehicle architecture — HVAC system on upper torso); attach cooling loop pressure gauge; measure flow rate indicator
steps.warning: 48V battery system — lockout before accessing any cooling circuit near battery

steps.action: Drain cooling loop via drain plug; flush with 1L deionized water; refill with Tesla-spec coolant (assumed OAT extended-life ethylene glycol or Tesla Novec-equivalent); bleed air
steps.tool: drain container, deionized water, Tesla-spec coolant, bleed tool
steps.warning: Use only manufacturer-specified coolant — incorrect chemistry causes aluminum corrosion in micro-channels

steps.action: Check cooling pump: measure pump motor current at startup vs spec; if >20% over baseline or pump fails to start within 2 seconds, replace pump assembly
After service: run full charge/discharge cycle; monitor cell temperature max — confirm <36°C throughout cycle in 22°C ambient

parts.part_name: Tesla OAT Extended-Life Coolant Concentrate 1L (diluted 50/50 with DI water)
Micro Cooling Pump DC 24V 5LPM (nearest commercial equivalent)

failure_modes.component: vision-based localization (FSD camera array — torso + head mounted)
failure_modes.symptom: robot loses position in manufacturing cell after lighting change, returns incorrect object pose for grasping task, repeated task abort events linked to 'pose uncertainty too high'
failure_modes.root_cause: FSD neural network localization degrading under factory lighting conditions not well-represented in training data (sodium vapor lamps, variable LED intensity); camera lens soiling from manufacturing environment particulates (metal chips, coolant mist); adversarial environment conditions causing distribution shift

repair_protocol.title: Camera Lens Cleaning and Visual Localization Reference Map Update

steps.action: Review task abort log for pattern: if aborts cluster around specific lighting state or time of day, flag for lighting environment remediation before camera service
Clean all camera lenses: remove dust cover (if present); apply 2 sprays Zeiss Lens Care solution to microfibre cloth; wipe each lens with single radial strokes
steps.tool: Zeiss Lens Care spray, microfibre cloths
steps.warning: Do not spray solution directly on lens — spray on cloth first; avoid anti-reflective coating damage

steps.action: Update visual localization reference map for the manufacturing cell: run 'map capture' mode — robot walks slowly around cell perimeter at 0.3 m/s; system captures new lighting-corrected visual fingerprint map
Test localization: drive robot to 10 known waypoints; measure position error with external tracker (laser tracker or AprilTag ground truth); max error 15mm in XY, 2° in yaw
steps.tool: laser tracker or AprilTag ground truth fixture, measuring tape

steps.action: If localization error persists: install supplementary structured light markers (retroreflective targets) at 2m height around cell; update localization to use marker-fusion mode

tools_required: laser tracker or AprilTag ground truth
